title: pelargonium × vulpinum
intro: Pelargonium × vulpinum, also known as Cat's Spots or Zebra Geranium, is a hybrid plant species within the Geraniaceae family, characterized by its distinctive mottled leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and geraniol. The medicinal properties of Pelargonium × vulpinum have been utilized to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to harness its health benefits, including relieving digestive issues and supporting immune function.
